content.text:
You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise.

Workspace map:
- The project repo is mounted at the burrow workspace root.
- /workspace/.canopy/agent.json is the rendered agent definition (warren seeded it).
- /workspace/.mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl holds the project's expertise records.
- /workspace/.seeds/issues.jsonl holds the project's issue queue.

Operating contract:
- Edit files in place. Run tests when relevant.
- Quality gates are terminal, not advisory. You are NOT done until the gate exits zero. Resolve the command in this order: `$WARREN_QUALITY_GATE` if set, otherwise the command documented in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, otherwise fall back to `bun run check:all` or `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test`. Run it before committing and again before reporting completion. Do not declare the task complete, hand off, or end the session with a red gate — fix failures (including lint warnings, which CI treats as errors) until it is green. If the gate is genuinely unfixable in this run, say so explicitly and leave the work open rather than claiming success.
- Use git as you normally would. Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream.
- Committing is mandatory, not the same as staging. `git add` ALONE IS NOT ENOUGH — you must run `git commit` so the work lands as a real commit. A run that ends with staged-but-uncommitted changes is treated as a FAILURE (`dropped_commit`), not a success. Before you report completion, run `git status`/`git log` and confirm your changes are in a commit, not just staged. The only exception is when you have genuinely made no file changes at all.
- Do not run `git push` yourself — warren handles the push host-side after the run terminates.

---

work on sd warren-e33f. use ml. commit when done.

# Project Expertise Manifest (via Mulch)

> Manifest mode lists available domains. Load records on demand with `ml prime <domain>` or `ml prime --files <path>`.

## Quick Reference

- `ml prime <domain>` — load full records for one domain
- `ml prime --files <path>` — load records relevant to specific files
- `ml search "<query>"` — search records across domains
- `ml record <domain> --type <type> --description "..."` — store an insight
- `ml learn` — discover what to record from changed files
- `ml sync` — validate, stage, and commit .mulch/ changes

# Seeds Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `sd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session

# Session Close Protocol

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. Close completed issues:    sd close <id1> <id2> ...
[ ] 2. File issues for remaining:  sd create --title "..."
[ ] 3. Run quality gates:          bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck
[ ] 4. Sync and push:              sd sync && git push
[ ] 5. Verify:                     git status (must show "up to date with origin")
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use seeds for ALL task tracking (`sd create`, `sd ready`, `sd close`)

## build (50 records, updated just now)

### Conventions
- [mx-a596b4] warden-digest (warren-4240): weekly cron at '0 5 * * 0' (05:00 UTC Sunday); role agents/warden-digest.md re-wakes Audit Warden conversation via POST /conversations/:id/re-wake if anchoring run is terminal, then posts synthesis message via POST /conversations/:id/messages. Scheduled after tastewatch (04:00 Sunday) to accumulate all auditor findings first.
- [mx-ee2f37] OpenAPI 3.1 schema guard (warren-b46b / pl-7b06 step 21): scripts/generate-openapi.ts reuses extractRoutes() from generate-docs.ts to derive docs/openapi.yaml from ROUTE_TABLE in src/server/handlers.ts. Path params (:id) become {id}, first path segment becomes the OpenAPI tag (healthz/readyz/version collapse under 'meta'), handler symbol becomes operationId, and ROUTE_TABLE leading // comments surface as operation.description. Request/response bodies are intentionally permissive in V1 — tighten incrementally. Wired via 'bun run gen:openapi' / 'bun run gen:openapi:check'; check:all appends gen:openapi:check after gen:docs:check. js-yaml (already a runtime dep) handles emission. [relates to: mx-c598e1]
- [mx-5cb52a] Warren version bump for the R-07 disk-seam closure landed as 0.2.0 (warren plan pl-a31c, parent warren-0a83) — the minor bump signals that warren↔burrow is now HTTP-only on every code path except reap's mulch_merge + branch_push. package.json + src/index.ts must move in lockstep (release.yml grep-checks); CHANGELOG.md 0.2.0 entry references burrow plan bur-pl-2467 as the burrow-side R-07 origin. [relates to: mx-3aac69]
- [mx-89e2c7] Handlers monolith split phase 2 (warren-a2b4 / pl-9088 step 2) — merged in commit b66f19b2. Extracted src/server/handlers/runs.ts (789 lines: listRunsHandler, getRunHandler, createRunHandler, postRunMessageHandler, steerRunHandler, cancelRunHandler, previewLoginHandler, previewTeardownHandler, streamRunEventsHandler, listCostAnalyticsHandler + streaming helpers bridgeAbort/asNdjsonStream/eventToNdjson) and src/server/handlers/plan-runs.ts (484 lines: createPlanRunHandler, listPlanRunsHandler, getPlanRunHandler, cancelPlanRunHandler, streamPlanRunEventsHandler + tailPlanRunEvents). Tests: src/server/handlers/runs.test.ts (988 lines) extracts POST /runs spawn/plot_id/interactive + /runs/:id/messages + GET /runs/:id/events describes from handlers.test.ts; src/server/handlers/plan-runs.test.ts (1318 lines) relocated from src/server/handlers.plan-runs.test.ts, biome noExcessiveLinesPerFunction override removed by splitting the monolithic POST /plan-runs describe into three siblings sharing setupPlanRunFixture(). Reconfirms playbook from phase 1 (mx-3df5c5) and phase 3 (mx-929bbc): ROUTE_TABLE stays in handlers/index.ts as canonical wire surface; shared parsing helpers exported from handlers/index.ts. [relates to: mx-3df5c5, mx-929bbc]
- [mx-eae53c] When a CHANGELOG entry overstates a release (e.g. R-13 in v0.3.2 announcing Postgres support that crash-looped on first repo call), amend the original release section with a brief blockquote 'Correction (date):' callout that points at the follow-up plan, then add the actual completion as a new [Unreleased] entry. The blockquote preserves the audit trail without rewriting history; the [Unreleased] entry is where the real fix is recorded. [relates to: mx-d1caf7]
- [mx-929bbc] Handlers monolith split phase 3 (warren-599c / pl-9088 step 3): src/server/handlers/index.ts → agents.ts (203 lines), projects.ts (261), burrows.ts (116), workers.ts (89), diagnostics.ts (136), meta.ts (42); handlers/index.ts shrinks from 1194 to 420 lines and now contains only the ROUTE_TABLE composer + shared parsing helpers + API_PREFIXES/isAuthExempt/buildApiRoutes. Pattern follows phase 1 (mx-3df5c5) and phase 2 (mx-99ad0d): shared helpers re-imported via './index.ts'; ROUTE_TABLE stays in handlers/index.ts as canonical wire surface for gen:docs and gen:openapi. Route handler symbol renames (listAgents→listAgentsHandler, healthz→healthzHandler, readyz→readyzHandler, version→versionHandler) propagate through docs/http-api.md + docs/openapi.yaml — must run 'bun run gen:docs && bun run gen:openapi' after the split. Tests reorganised: handlers.test.ts → handlers/agents.test.ts + handlers/projects.test.ts + handlers/brainstorm.test.ts; handlers.preview.test.ts → handlers/index.test.ts (isAuthExempt only) + handlers/meta.test.ts (/preview/config) + handlers/runs.preview.test.ts (login + teardown + proxy preamble); handlers.burrows.test.ts and handlers.workers.test.ts move under handlers/ without splitting. agents.ts handles BOTH GET/POST /agents routes AND POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh since projectCanopyClient + refreshProjectAgents are the agents subsystem. diagnostics.ts owns /readyz with private check helpers; meta.ts holds inert /healthz + /version + /preview/config probes. Removed handlers/index.ts from biome's noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity override list; added handlers/projects.ts (runProjectTriggerHandler stays complex at 22). [relates to: mx-99ad0d]
- [mx-8f151f] warren 0.4.1 patch-bumps the burrow-cli double-pin consumer-side update for burrow-59cd (body.env parsing on POST /burrows). CHANGELOG entry must name burrow-59cd as the unblocker and call out the scenario 25 flip from soft-skipped to hard-required for the env-reaches-sandbox / workspace plot append / plot.* mirror assertions. Pattern: when the warren patch closes a cross-repo gap (burrow fix → warren consumer bump), the CHANGELOG entry describes the gap, names the burrow seed id, and lists the three changed locations (double-pin, scenario flip, version bump in two places per mx-70e9e9).
- [mx-ed7861] Plots handlers split phase 2 (warren-332b / pl-369d): src/server/handlers/plots.ts (1010 lines) deleted; remaining handlers extracted into plots/ domain files — detail.ts (GET /plots/:id + /summary), intent.ts (intent + rename), status.ts (status), attachments.ts (attach/detach/merge-PR). Shared helpers in plots/shared.ts: resolvePlotProject(deps,plotId,action) throws byte-identical 404/ProjectLacksPlotError; buildPlotEnvelope; loadPausedRunsForPlot. plots/index.ts is now a pure re-export composer. plots.test.ts (2399 lines) split into plots.{detail,intent,rename,status,attach,detach,merge}.test.ts sharing plots.test-support.ts (non-.test.ts helper). attach/merge handlers gained parseAttachmentBody/parseMergeMethod helpers to drop under cognitive-complexity 15, so the old plots.ts biome grandfather entry was removed (not relocated); file-size budgets for plots.ts + plots.test.ts removed. [relates to: mx-3df5c5]
- [mx-f1f5f7] Bundle-size ratchet (warren-5abc) has a documented escape hatch for foundational growth: when a Phase legitimately expands the surface (e.g. warren-23fe Phase 1 design tokens added the status palette, radius/shadow/z scales, brand color, and self-hosted font CSS), raise the floors in scripts/bundle-size-budgets.json with an inline $comment_<bucket>_<date> field naming the seed + plan step + what was added + what was trimmed to minimise the bump + the named child seeds that must hold flat against the new floor. This mirrors the file-size grandfather pattern: a one-time legitimate raise with a tracker rationale, not a silent ratchet weakening. Don't use this for casual additions or dep bumps — those still owe a code-split or trim. For warren-23fe CSS went raw 34474->35200 (+726B) / gzip 6960->7160 (+200B); we trimmed unused -foreground pairs (success/warning/info/danger/neutral/card/accent), --shadow-xl, and --z-base to keep the bump minimal. [relates to: mx-3c5052]
- [mx-c598e1] X-Request-ID middleware (warren-30af / pl-7b06 step 19): src/server/request-id.ts owns the correlation-id seam. extractOrGenerateRequestId(request) honours an inbound X-Request-ID when it matches /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]{1,128}$/ (narrow whitelist; rejects CRLF and log-spoof bytes) else mints crypto.randomUUID(). server.ts's fetchHandler wraps every dispatch: extracts/mints the id, builds a child logger via bindRequestIdLogger (uses pino .child when present else a manual shim injecting request_id), threads both into handleRequest, then calls stampRequestId on the returned Response so X-Request-ID is uniformly set on success/error/auth-deny/404/405/UI-fallback. RouteContext gained requestId:string so handlers can propagate downstream. Coverage in src/server/request-id.test.ts (unit) + src/server/request-id.wire.test.ts (e2e via startServer). Wire tests live in a sibling file because src/server/server.test.ts is at its frozen file-size budget — extend siblings rather than growing server.test.ts.
- [mx-e078d8] Drizzle migration meta files (src/db/migrations/meta/NNNN_snapshot.json, _journal.json) emitted by drizzle-kit generate are indented with two spaces by default, but biome.json formats them with tabs. After 'bun run db:generate', run 'bun run biome format --write src/db/migrations/meta/NNNN_snapshot.json src/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json' or 'bun run lint' will fail. Also format the generated 0008_snapshot.json the same way. (warren-135b)
- [mx-6915a8] warren-ace1 / pl-da5b step 7 validation: bun run check:all is fully green end-to-end provided node_modules is populated (bun install first). Stale node_modules symptom: scripts/check-ci-parity.test.ts cannot resolve js-yaml at test time, contributes 0 covered functions, All-files functions coverage drops from ~86% to 83.22% (below 85% floor), check:coverage exits with a misleading 'add tests' message. Class B regression (missing-artifact reference in AGENTS.md) is reliably caught by 'bun run validate:agents-md' inside check:all: appending a backticked nonexistent path produces a [missing-path] failure and non-zero exit. Class A regression (postgres FK schema-qualified as public.projects) is mirrored locally by 'bun run test:pg' (NOT in check:all because it boots a docker container); when docker is absent the script exits loudly with an install-docker hint rather than skipping. Operator runbook: before tagging a release that touches src/db/migrations/postgres/, run 'bun run test:pg' on a docker-capable host; CI ci-postgres.yml is the merge gate. [relates to: mx-cedf5d, mx-6140f1]
- [mx-fc127e] 'bun run lint' (biome check --error-on-warnings .) descends into .claude/worktrees/<branch>/ and fails with 'Found a nested root configuration' when agent worktrees exist locally — each worktree has its own biome.json. Workaround: run 'bunx biome check --error-on-warnings src/ scripts/ drizzle.config.ts' to scan explicit paths and bypass the nested-root detection. Pre-existing local-environment condition; biome.json on main is unchanged.
- [mx-bc5079] Release patch flow (warren-325d / pl-5516 step 3) per .claude/commands/release.md: edit package.json version + src/index.ts VERSION in lockstep (release.yml fails if they disagree), move [Unreleased] entries to a new dated section in CHANGELOG.md, then run 'bun run gen:openapi' — docs/openapi.yaml's info.version is sourced from package.json, so gen:openapi:check fails check:all on drift. No CLAUDE.md/README.md edits needed for a pure patch with no command-surface change. Also: a fresh workspace needs 'bun install' before 'bun run check:all' — otherwise module-resolving tests fail (js-yaml, @os-eco/burrow-cli, @os-eco/plot-cli, drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite) and drag function coverage to ~83%, below the 85% floor. [relates to: mx-92eaee]
- [mx-3f03bd] Adding a drizzle migration: edit schema.ts → bun run db:generate emits src/db/migrations/NNNN_<random-codename>.sql + meta/NNNN_snapshot.json + appends to _journal.json. Then (1) rename the SQL to a descriptive NNNN_<verb_noun>.sql, (2) update the matching 'tag' in meta/_journal.json so the migrator can find it, (3) run 'bunx biome format --write' on the new snapshot + journal — drizzle-kit emits 2-space indent but biome.json enforces tabs.
- [mx-3c5052] UI build entrypoint from repo root: 'bun run build:ui' shells 'cd src/ui && bun install && bun run build'. Output lands at src/ui/dist/{index.html, assets/*}. Server picks it up via WARREN_UI_DIST_DIR (defaults to <cwd>/src/ui/dist via src/server/config.ts:resolveUiDistDir). UI handler (src/server/ui.ts) refuses /agents, /projects, /runs, /healthz, /readyz prefixes and falls through to index.html for any other GET so the SPA can render deep links.
- [mx-384e1f] CI runs 'bun run check:deps' (knip 6.x in --dependencies mode) via knip.json (warren-d109 / pl-7b06 step 12). Covers root package and the src/ui workspace; CI runs 'bun run ui:install' before check:deps so knip can resolve src/ui imports. Findings are almost always real - fix with 'bun remove <dep>' (or 'cd src/ui && bun remove <dep>'); only ignore runtime/string-resolved peers (e.g. pino transports loaded by string name). Initial wiring removed chalk and pino-pretty from the root package. [relates to: mx-27cfb6]
